29 May 2026 13:53
RNS Number : 2909G
Trustpilot Group PLC
29 May 2026
29 May 2026
Trustpilot Group plc
(the "Company")
Total voting rights
The following information is disclosed in accordance with DTR 5.6.1R of the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules of the Financial Conduct Authority ("
FCA
").
As at the date of this announcement, the Company's issued ordinary share capital consists of 387,573,556 ordinary shares of £0.01 each (the "
Ordinary Shares
"), each carrying the right to one vote at a general meeting of the Company. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury.
Therefore, the total number of voting rights attributable to the Ordinary Shares is 387,573,556.
The figure in the preceding sentence may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Ordinary Shares under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

2:
While pleased that all resolutions at the AGM were approved by shareholders, the Board of Directors of Trustpilot acknowledges that the vote on Resolution 2 received less than 80% support (76.04%). Following the AGM, the Company will continue to engage with its shareholders and carefully consider all feedback. In line with the UK Corporate Governance Code, we will publish an update on our shareholder engagement within six months of the 2026 AGM.
As at 15 May 2026, the Company's issued ordinary share capital consisted of 388,193,856 ordinary shares of £0.01 each, each carrying the right to one vote at a general meeting of the Company.
In accordance with UK Listing Rule 6.4.2R, copies of the resolutions passed as special business at the AGM have been submitted to the National Storage Mechanism and will shortly be available for inspection at
https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism
.
The resolutions carried at the AGM are set out in full in the Notice of Annual General Meeting which can be found on the Company's website at investors.trustpilot.com

latest.title: CEO’s Comments at the 2026 Annual General Meeting
latest.content:
19 May 2026 13:00
RNS Number : 9073E
Trustpilot Group PLC
19 May 2026
Trustpilot plc
19 May 2026
CEO's Comments at the 2026 Annual General Meeting
Trustpilot today releases the comments made by CEO Adrian Blair at the 2026 Annual General Meeting, held in London at 1pm today.
Good afternoon, I'm Adrian Blair, CEO of Trustpilot. A warm welcome to all of you.
2025 was another excellent year for Trustpilot - one that demonstrated why, as the world's largest open customer feedback platform, the role we play is becoming more important than ever in the age of AI.
Trustpilot helps people make choices and make themselves heard; and we help businesses build trust, grow and improve by engaging with customer feedback.
The rise of AI is increasing the value of that feedback - so Trustpilot is uniquely positioned to benefit. AI click-throughs to our platform grew nearly fifteen-fold year on year, and Trustpilot was ranked the fifth most cited domain globally on ChatGPT in January 2026. Businesses increasingly need Trustpilot to compete for visibility in the AI-powered digital landscape.
Strong execution delivered record financial results. Bookings grew 18 percent in constant currency to 291 million dollars. Revenue reached 261 million dollars. Adjusted EBITDA grew 69 percent to 40.7 million dollars, a margin of 15.6 percent, up over 4 percentage points year on year.
The consumer flywheel continues to power the business. Active reviews on the platform grew 20 percent to 361 million. More reviews were written on Trustpilot in 2025 than in the first twelve years of the company combined.
We also returned 71.6 million dollars to shareholders through our buyback programme, and announced a further 30 million dollar buyback with our results in March.
The momentum in the business, and tailwind of AI gives us immense confidence in the years ahead. We have upgraded our medium-term margin targets to 25 percent adjusted EBITDA by 2028, and 30 percent by 2030.
I would like to close by thanking Trusties around the world for their extraordinary commitment, and our shareholders for their continued support.
Thank you.
Notice of trading update
The Group will report its first half trading update on 16 July 2026.
Contacts
Trustpilot Group plc
Louise Bryant, Head of Investor Relations
Priyal Soni, Investor Relations
investor.relations@trustpilot.com
+44 (0) 7813 210 809
+44 (0) 7391 859 625
Headland Consultancy
Stephen Malthouse
Rob Walker
Charlie Pepper
trustpilot@headlandconsultancy.com
Tel: +44 (0) 20 3805 4822
About Trustpilot
Trustpilot began in 2007 with a simple yet powerful idea that is more relevant today than ever - to be the universal symbol of trust, bringing consumers and businesses together through reviews. Trustpilot is open, independent, and impartial - we help consumers make the right choices and businesses to build trust, grow and improve.
Today, we have more than 361 million reviews with 160 billion annual Trustbox impressions, and the numbers keep growing. We have more than 1,000 employees and we're headquartered in Copenhagen, with operations in Amsterdam, Denver, Edinburgh, Hamburg, London, Melbourne, Milan and New York.

company_30s.what_they_do: Trustpilot runs a global review platform that lets customers rate and review businesses.  It also sells subscription services that give companies analytics, reputation management tools, and marketing insights based on those reviews.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits at the centre of online consumer decision‑making.  Millions of shoppers read Trustpilot reviews before buying, so the platform wields a lot of influence over retail and service sectors.  Its data‑analytics arm turns that traffic into a recurring revenue stream for businesses that want to understand and improve their customer experience.
company_30s.current_state: Trustpilot has just finished a strong 2025 earnings season, announced a share‑buyback programme, and brought on a new CFO.  The company is still trading at a steep discount to its pre‑crash valuation, but the latest moves suggest it is stabilising and may be positioning for a recovery.

rns_narrative: Corporate governance and capital‑market activity: new director appointments, a buy‑back declaration, and a transaction in the company’s own shares point to a focus on shareholder value.
Financial health and outlook: the 2025 annual report shows profitability ahead of expectations, and the upcoming AGM may reveal further buy‑back or dividend plans.
Market engagement: the company is hosting a trust‑education event and has a recent secondary placing, signalling efforts to broaden its investor base and strengthen market presence.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter focuses on the solid 2025 results, the active buy‑back, and the new CFO’s track record.  Some investors see the education event as a sign that the brand is building a stronger community.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the lingering volatility, the fact that the stock remains far below its all‑time high, and the risk of further dilution from share‑buyback activity.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has drifted from indifferent to slightly bullish as more positive company announcements surface, but engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, which means the market has largely ignored the stock and any news could generate a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: The AGM on 16 April 2026 could confirm additional buy‑back or dividend plans, giving the stock a clear support level.
Q2 2026 earnings (likely in June) will test whether the profitability trend continues.
A new product launch or partnership with a major retailer could broaden the customer base and add a new revenue stream.
A sudden volume spike during any of the above events would signal renewed institutional interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained volume surge coupled with a breakout above the 200‑day moving average on the daily chart.  A bullish engulfing or a short‑term reversal pattern would be a strong confirmation.
expect_next.timing_regime: The price is still in the late phase of a deep crash; compression is tight, so a mean‑reversion is likely once a catalyst lifts sentiment.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has fallen far below its historic peak, creating a steep upside window.  A bounce to the midpoint of the all‑time high would double the current price, and reaching the former peak would triple it.  The recent buy‑back and strong earnings give a solid foundation for a recovery.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Continued volatility could keep the price depressed, and further dilution from the buy‑back program or regulatory challenges could erode value.  The worst‑case scenario is a further 10‑15% drop from the current level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 200‑400% if the stock recovers to the midpoint or peak.  Potential downside: 70% of the historic peak.  The reward outweighs the risk by a factor of at least 3:1.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price closes above the recent resistance and volume exceeds the 3‑month average.  Use 10% of the portfolio, place a stop 20% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a clear volume spike at the 200‑day moving average or for the Q2 earnings release.  A breakout above the 200‑MA with at least 1.5× average daily volume would be the trigger.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Trustpilot’s deep crash, combined with a strong earnings beat and an active buy‑back, sets up a high‑asymmetry play: a 200‑400% upside is possible against a 70% downside risk if the company’s recovery catalyst materialises.
